task_completion.description: Verify the 'Regression Plan' was renamed to 'v1.0 Release Plan'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Azure DevOps Test Plans hub, the root test plan in the left sidebar or main header is named 'v1.0 Release Plan'.

task_completion.description: Verify a Static Suite named 'Checkout Module' was created
task_completion.visual_evidence: A suite/folder named 'Checkout Module' is visible in the left sidebar under the 'v1.0 Release Plan'.

task_completion.description: Verify the required test cases were added to the 'Checkout Module' suite
task_completion.visual_evidence: When the 'Checkout Module' suite is selected, the main pane lists the test cases: 'Checkout with Credit Card', 'Checkout with PayPal', 'Apply Discount Code', and 'Refund Order'.

task_completion.description: Verify a Query-based Suite named 'Smoke Tests' was created
task_completion.visual_evidence: A suite named 'Smoke Tests' (typically with a query/filter icon) is visible in the left sidebar under the 'v1.0 Release Plan'.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Smoke Tests' query is configured to include Test Cases with Priority = 1
task_completion.visual_evidence: The query editor for 'Smoke Tests' shows a clause where 'Priority' equals '1', or the resulting test case list explicitly shows only Priority 1 test cases.

integrity.description: The agent used the Azure DevOps web interface to perform the tasks
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Azure DevOps web UI in a web browser, rather than terminal commands, API scripts, or code editors.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual application UI elements
integrity.visual_evidence: The names 'v1.0 Release Plan', 'Checkout Module', and 'Smoke Tests' appear in the actual Azure DevOps Test Plans UI elements (sidebars, lists, headers), not just typed into a notepad or search bar.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the UI state
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser developer tools (F12/Inspect) are not open in the screenshots, and the UI appears to be a standard, unmanipulated Azure DevOps page.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Test Plan Name: 'v1.0 Release Plan'. Expected Static Suite Name: 'Checkout Module'. Expected Test Cases in Static Suite: 'Checkout with Credit Card', 'Checkout with PayPal', 'Apply Discount Code', 'Refund Order'. Expected Query-based Suite Name: 'Smoke Tests'. Expected Query Filter: Priority = 1.
